text: about 22% of the infants are born with 3low birth-weight (<2500 g) , as compared to less than 10% in the developed countries. Both clinical and sub-clinical undernutrition are widely prevalent even during early childhood and adolescence. Though the prevalence of florid forms of severe PEM like kwashiorkor and marasmus among preschool children is <1 %, national surveys indicate that about half of (48%) <5 year children suffer from sub- clinical undernutrition such as underweight (43%) stunting and wasting (20%) which 3indicates that undernutrition is of long duration . The studies have shown that there is a steep increase in the prevalence of underweight with increase of age, from 27% 4at 6 months of age to a high of 45% at 24 months of age .
